title: Quiz: Quantum Federated Protocols
description: Test your understanding of quantum-specific federated learning protocols

questions.question: Why is circular averaging needed for quantum parameters?

options.text: Quantum parameters are periodic (e.g., angles in [0, 2π])

questions.explanation: Quantum rotation parameters are periodic. Standard averaging of values near 0 and 2π gives π, but circular averaging correctly gives a value near 0.

questions.question: In heterogeneous quantum FL, what factors should influence aggregation weights?
questions.explanation: Aggregation should consider both data size (as in standard FL) and device quality factors like fidelity and connectivity.

questions.question: What is staleness in asynchronous federated learning?

options.text: The gap between client's training version and current global version
The age of the quantum device

questions.explanation: Staleness measures how outdated a client's update is relative to the current global model version.

questions.question: In noise-aware aggregation, noisier clients should receive higher weights.
questions.explanation: False. Noise-aware aggregation weights updates inversely to noise, giving lower weight to noisier (less reliable) clients.

questions.question: How does secure aggregation protect privacy?

options.text: By using masks that cancel when aggregated

questions.explanation: Secure aggregation uses pairwise masks that sum to zero, so individual updates are hidden but the aggregate is revealed.

questions.question: When should asynchronous federated learning be preferred?

options.text: When all devices have identical performance
When connectivity is poor or device speeds vary greatly
When privacy is the top priority

questions.explanation: Async FL is preferred when devices have varying speeds or connectivity issues, avoiding the bottleneck of waiting for slow clients.

questions.question: What happens to very stale updates in async FL?
questions.explanation: Very stale updates are typically rejected or given very low weight because they were computed on an outdated global model.

questions.question: Which are challenges specific to quantum federated learning? (Select all)

options.text: Varying noise profiles across quantum devices
Limited qubit counts on some devices

questions.explanation: Quantum FL faces periodic parameters, device-specific noise, and hardware constraints. Data being IID would make FL easier, not harder.
